mnemonics.title: Format Specifiers Made Easy
mnemonics.content: D-F-S-C-L-U-X-G-P: %d for int, %f for float, %s for string, %c for char, %ld for long, %u for unsigned, %x for hex, %g for float, %p for pointer. REMEMBER: DOLLAR SIGN CLEARS UNIQUE GROUPS PURPLE.

mnemonics.title: Escape Sequences Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: NEW TABBED CARRIAGE RETURN BELLS FORM FEED VERTICAL: \n=\t=\r=\b=\a=\f=\v. SAY NO TRUCK REPAIR BACK BEFORE ALARM FIXING VAN.

mnemonics.title: scanf Address Rule
mnemonics.content: WHEN USING SCANF, ALWAYS USE & FOR VARIABLES. FOR ARRAYS (STRINGS), NO & NEEDED. THINK: & MEANS 'WHERE', NOT 'WHAT'.

acronyms.usage: stdio.h is the standard library header for input and output functions in C
A special value returned by getchar() and scanf() to indicate no more input is available
The 'f' in printf stands for formatted, meaning it uses format specifiers to control output

visualTips.visualization: IMAGINE PRINTF AS A STAMP: THE FORMAT STRING IS THE STAMP DESIGN, AND ARGUMENTS ARE THE INK COLORS THAT FILL IN THE BLANKS.
THINK OF & AS A MAILBOX SIGN. scanf() NEEDS TO KNOW THE ADDRESS (MAILBOX LOCATION) TO DELIVER (STORE) THE INPUT VALUE.
THE BACKSLASH \ IS A 'TRANSLATOR' THAT TELLS THE COMPILER: 'THE NEXT CHARACTER IS SPECIAL, NOT LITERAL.'

analogies.analogy: PRINTF IS LIKE A PRINTER WITH TEMPLATES. THE FORMAT STRING IS A TEMPLATE WITH PLACEHOLDERS, AND ARGUMENTS FILL IN THE BLANKS.
FORMAT SPECIFIERS ARE LIKE PLACEHOLDERS IN A FORM. %d IS A 'NUMBER BLANK', %s IS A 'WORD BLANK', %f IS A 'DECIMAL BLANK'.
\n IS LIKE HITTING ENTER TO START A NEW LINE. \t IS LIKE PRESSING TAB TO MOVE TO THE NEXT COLUMN STOP.
THE INPUT BUFFER IS LIKE A CONVEYOR BELT. scanf() READS FROM IT LIKE A MACHINE PICKING ITEMS. IF NOT ALL IS USED, IT REMAINS FOR THE NEXT SCANF().
